feat: compute real vault liquidity from market allocations

Liquidity = Σ min(vault_allocation_in_market, market_available_liquidity).
Matches the value shown on app.morpho.org (verified: $162.2M vs $162.51M).
Also derives utilizationRate = deployed / totalAssets for vault rows.

Fix deploy: stop+kill port before start, trigger on workflow changes

systemctl restart was causing "Address already in use" because the old
uvicorn process hadn't released port 8000. Now we stop, wait, kill the
port as a safety net, then start. Also trigger pipeline on workflow
file changes so deploy fixes don't require a backend file touch.

Classify DefaultSkew_NumericLiterals → §14.4 [skip ci]

The test exercises the `default input #skew output #skew` declaration inside a clocking block with numeric literal skew values (#3 and #7). §14.4 "Input and output skews" defines the clocking_skew syntax and semantics, including the default_skew form that sets default timing for all signals in the block.

commit.message:
fix(ci): tune thresholds, fix FP parsing, exclude Windows builds

- Set Go thresholds based on actual CI results: 25K+ unit, 40+
  integration, 80+ e2e, 200+ security tests, 70% coverage minimum
- Fix grep -oE multi-line result parsing in false-positive-check.sh
  by using sed to extract values instead of nested grep
- Exclude Windows/amd64 from CI builds (syscall.Statfs_t unavailable
  for cross-compilation, known issue)
- Add benchtime=1s and timeout 1200s guard for benchmark step to
  prevent pathologically slow benchmarks from blocking pipeline

commit.message:
perf: optimize shade-walk Dijkstra + fix errors

- Cap half_budget in find_shade_loop to min(1.2×target, 8000m) — prevents
  300-min walks from exploring full graph (61s→31s improvement)
- Cap target_dist_dijkstra to bbox physical size (bbox_radius × 2.8) so
  waypoints don't fall outside the street network bbox
- Fix fetchShadeWalk Android: handle {"detail":...} HTTP errors gracefully
  instead of crashing with JSONException "no value for routes"
- Add Overpass mirror fallback (overpass.kumi.systems) + stale cache fallback
- Fix undefined _urban_canyon_shade_fast → use _point_physics_shade
- Fix shade_fraction=0% at night: use Dijkstra-based estimate as fallback
- Extend duration chips to 300 min with smart labels (ч/мин format)
- MIN_SEG_M 0.5→3.0m to filter micro-segments (3-4× graph size reduction)

Performance: 20min=10s, 60min=27s, 120min=10s(cached), 300min=31s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

for consistent formatting across all results in the same event.

commit.message:
Classify DefaultSkew_NoEdgeEvent → §14.4 [skip ci]

The test exercises a clocking block with `default input #1step output negedge`, which declares default skew values for input and output signals. IEEE 1800-2023 §14.4 (Input and output skews) is the specific subclause covering skew definitions, including the `default_skew` grammar rule and how `#1step` and edge-based skews apply to clocking block signals.

commit.message:
fix(cwd): canonicalize $HOME before path comparison

On Fedora, /home is a symlink to /var/home. validate_cwd compared the
canonicalized cwd (/var/home/...) against raw $HOME (/home/...) which
never matched, silently disabling code and plan gates.

commit.message:
Fix form export for XFA-only PDFs with direct page drawing fallback

IRS forms are often XFA-only with no AcroForm fields, so pdf-lib's
form API finds nothing. Now stores field geometry (canvas coords,
pageNum, fieldType) in each edit, and when the form API fails,
draws text/checkmarks directly onto the PDF pages using the stored
positions converted to PDF coordinates.

commit.message:
feat: settings menu + smart headlines + i18n menu labels

Backend:
- User model: add bio (Text) and pronouns (String 50), both nullable
- Alembic migration 3a7f82c1d905: adds bio + pronouns columns to users table
- schemas/user.py: UserResponse now includes bio + pronouns; new UpdateProfileRequest schema
- PATCH /api/v1/auth/me: update display_name, bio, pronouns, preferred_lang
- GET /api/v1/articles/headlines: prioritises global/national scale articles
  from the past 7 days (limit 5), falls back to recent articles

Frontend:
- SettingsMenu (new): hamburger ≡ icon in TopBar (logged-in only); dropdown
  with Account section (display_name, email read-only, bio, pronouns, Save)
  and Language section (Default / English / Chinese radio, saves to backend
  and navigates URL locale on change)
- TopBar: integrates SettingsMenu; adds useEffect to sync user's preferred_lang
  to URL locale on login (en → /en, zh → /zh, default → no nav)
- AuthProvider: adds refreshUser() to re-fetch /me after profile edits
- MarketTicker: HeadlineTicker now calls fetchHeadlines() instead of
  fetchArticles(), showing only high-impact global/national events (max 5)
- MenuBar: section labels now come from i18n messages (menu.*) so they
  switch automatically with the URL locale
- messages/en.json + zh.json: add full settings.* translation keys;
  update menu.* to match current backend sections (technology, energy)
- api.ts: add updateProfile() and fetchHeadlines()
- types/index.ts: User interface gains optional bio + pronouns fields

pub/sub with 10 event topics and 100-event ring buffer.

audit (20 findings, 1 fixed, 2 acceptable for alpha, rest tracked).

Build: 0 errors, 0 warnings. Tests: 979 Deno + 28 Rust = 1007 total.

commit.message:
feat(phase-4c): KI-Berichte im Admin mit WYSIWYG-Editor (#18)

* release: Phase 4a – Dropdown-Navigation, Zeitraum-Slider, Temperaturdaten (#16)

* feat(simulation): Klimasimulations-Engine mit RCP-Szenarien (#9)

Implementiert vollständige Simulationslogik:
- SimulationEngine mit linearer CO₂-Projektion (IPCC-Formel)
- Strahlungsantrieb (5.35 × ln(C/C₀)) und Temperaturanomalie (ECS)
- Alle RCP-Szenarien (2.6/4.5/6.0/8.5) + CUSTOM
- compare_scenarios() für Szenario-Vergleich
- API-Router auf SimulationEngine umgestellt
- 36 Unit-Tests, 100% Coverage

Co-authored-by: loschi1982 <loschwitz@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(visualization): Dashboard-Erweiterung mit 3 neuen Komponenten (#11)

- SimulationChart: RCP-Szenario-Vergleich (alle 4 Szenarien, Recharts LineChart)
- StatsPanel: CO₂-Statistiken als Karten (Trend, Max, Mittel, Anomalien)
- AnomalyChart: Z-Score-Ausreißer als Scatter-Plot mit Severity-Farben
- App.jsx: Tab-Navigation (CO₂-Verlauf / Szenarien / Statistiken / Anomalien)
- API client: fetchCo2Stats, fetchAnomalies, compareScenarios ergänzt
- 32 Frontend-Tests (Vitest + React Testing Library), alle grün

Co-authored-by: loschi1982 <loschwitz@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(admin): passwortgeschützte Admin-Oberfläche (#13)

- LoginForm mit sessionStorage-Auth (admin/1234)
- DataSourcesManager: Datenquellen anzeigen + per Klick importieren
- DatasetsOverview: importierte Datensätze als Tabelle
- Admin-Tab (⚙) in der Tab-Navigation
- API client: fetchSources, ingestSource, fetchDatasets ergänzt
- 12 neue Tests (44 Frontend-Tests gesamt)
- Port-Fix in Co2Chart-Fehlermeldung (8000→8001)

Co-authored-by: loschi1982 <loschwitz@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(phase-4a): Dropdown-Navigation, Zeitraum-Slider, Temperaturdaten, klickbare Quellen (#15)

Backend:
- NASA GISS (1880–heute) als neue Datenquelle mit GISS-CSV-Parser
- source_url-Feld in allen DataSource-Einträgen
- Generischer /api/v1/analysis/series/{source_id} Endpunkt
- /api/v1/analysis/temperature Endpunkt (NASA GISS)

Frontend:
- NavBar: Dropdown-Navigation mit Hover-Untermenüs (Klimadaten/Temperaturen/Analyse)
- TimeRangeSlider: Dual-Handle Slider für variablen Zeitraum (1880–heute)
- TemperatureChart: Temperaturanomalie mit +1.5°C/+2.0°C Referenzlinien
- ClimateSeriesChart: Generischer Chart für CO₂ (Mauna Loa, Global) und CH₄
- Klickbare Quellenangaben (NOAA GML, NASA GISS, IPCC) im Footer und Charts
- AppContext: geteilter Zustand für Zeitraum und aktuelle Ansicht
- 62 Frontend-Tests (alle grün), 167 Python-Tests (alle grün)

Co-authored-by: loschi1982 <loschwitz@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Classify DefaultSkew_PerSignalOverride → §14.4 [skip ci]

The test exercises a clocking block with a default skew (`default input #10ns output #2ns`) and per-signal skew overrides (`output negedge ack`, `input #1step addr`). §14.4 "Input and output skews" is the dedicated subclause covering skew specification on clocking block signals, including 1step and per-signal overrides.

commit.message:
Gobii agent: pre-flight URL check, timeout reduction, batch limit, platform rules

- Pre-flight URL check: follow redirects before dispatching task, skip Workday/Taleo/iCIMS early
- Default LIMIT changed from 1 to 5 (batch cap to avoid overloading Gobii queue)
- Poll timeout reduced from 45 min to 15 min (900s); interval 20s → 15s
- Platform PROCEED rules extended: Glassdoor, ZipRecruiter, Wellfound added
- Fix: invalid 'Requires Manual' Airtable status replaced with 'Pending Review'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: progressive disclosure structure, add components + performance docs

Restructure CLAUDE.md (57→65 lines) with task→doc routing table.
Apply progressive disclosure: root file stays under 70 lines,
detailed knowledge lives in domain-specific docs.

New docs:
- docs/components.md — shared components, retention features, state patterns
- docs/performance.md — CSS animation rules, render efficiency, DB patterns

Updated docs:
- docs/games.md — reflect shared components, event dispatch pattern
- docs/gotchas.md — add new patterns, remove perf info (moved to performance.md)
- docs/api.md — trim duplicated caching info (cross-ref performance.md)

perf(collaborate-demo): ref-based cursor rendering, zero re-renders on messages

Per Vercel React best practices (rerender-use-ref-transient-values):
- Cursor positions stored in refs, rendered via direct DOM manipulation
- No React re-renders on incoming MQTT messages (was 33-60 per second)
- Stats (latency, msg count) synced to state at 1Hz for header display
- USER_CREDENTIALS hoisted outside component (rendering-hoist-jsx)
- Removed dimensions/now state that caused unnecessary re-renders

commit.message:
Fix topbar stuck visible: start hide timer on all status changes

showBar starts true but the 4s hide timer only fired on 'playing'.
During loading/buffering no timer ran → bar stayed visible forever.
Fix: call bumpBar() on every non-error status change, and also on
fullscreenchange so entering fullscreen shows bar briefly then hides.

commit.message:
feat: dedicated economic_events table for FRED calendar data

Replaces generic api_cache blob with a proper queryable table.

supabase_cache.py:
- Add economic_events table to _MIGRATE_SQL (series_id, event_name,
  release_date, release_time, country, category, impact, actual,
  previous, unit, source, fetched_at; UNIQUE on series_id+release_date)
- Add upsert_economic_events(rows) and get_economic_events(from, to,
  min_fetched_at) CRUD helpers

fred_calendar.py:
- Add series_id field to all events (FRED series key or 'FOMC')
- Add _event_to_row() / _row_to_event() mapping helpers
- Rewrite _load_raw_events(): L1 memory → L2 economic_events table
  (fresh rows, TTL 6h) → L3 FRED API + upsert → stale DB fallback → mock
- Released events (actual IS NOT NULL) never re-fetched; upcoming
  events refreshed at most once per 6 hours regardless of traffic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

filtering, ride scoring, wishlist tracking, Single Rider folding, LL
return window display, and enriched clipboard export with prompt wrapper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify DefaultSkew_1StepInputNegedgeOutput → §14.4 [skip ci]

The test exercises the `default input #1step output negedge` declaration inside a clocking block, which specifies default input and output skews. IEEE 1800-2023 §14.4 "Input and output skews" defines this syntax, including the `default` clocking skew directive and the use of `#1step` and edge identifiers (e.g., `negedge`) as skew values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
